chapter_title.ar: باب طَلَاقِ الْعَبْدِ إِذَا تَزَوَّجَ بِإِذْنِ مَوْلَاهُ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 75 – Divorce of the slave when he married by the permission of his master

text: سَأَلْتُ أَبَا عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) عَنِ الْعَبْدِ هَلْ يَجُوزُ طَلَاقُهُ فَقَالَ إِنْ كَانَتْ أَمَتَكَ فَلَا إِنَّ اللَّهَ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ يَقُولُ عَبْداً مَمْلُوكاً لا يَقْدِرُ عَلى شَيْ‏ءٍ وَ إِنْ كَانَتْ أَمَةَ قَوْمٍ آخَرِينَ أَوْ حُرَّةً جَازَ طَلَاقُهُ .

en.hubeali: Muhammad, from Ahmad, from Ibn Fazzal Bin Salih, from Lays Al Murady who said,

‘I asked Abu Abdullahasws about the slave, is his divorce allowed?’ So heasws said: ‘If she was a bondmaid, so no. Allahazwj Mighty and Majestic is Saying [16:75] a slave, the property of another, (who) has no power over anything. If she was a bondmaid of another people, or a free woman, his divorce is allowed’.
